title: Orchidium arcticum
intro: Orchidium arcticum, also known as the "Arctic Orchid," is a plant species native to the Arctic tundra. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including colds, coughs, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Orchidium arcticum include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Orchidium arcticum is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or decoction, and is also used in modern herbal supplements to support respiratory health and immune function.
taxonomy: Orchidium arcticum is a genus of orchids and more specifically, it is a synonym of the genus Pleione. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which comprises of orchids. The genus Pleione, to which Orchidium arcticum belongs, is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. Pleione is further divided into several species, each having distinct characteristics. Orchidium arcticum's classification is closely related to the classification of its synonym, the genus Pleione.
